title: Linode tokens should not be disclosed
description: Linode Personal Access Tokens (PATs) provide programmatic access to the Linode API, allowing management of compute instances, storage, networking, and other cloud infrastructure resources. When these tokens are hardcoded in source code, configuration files, or version control systems, they create critical security vulnerabilities. Exposed tokens can allow unauthorized users to create, modify, or delete cloud infrastructure, access sensitive data, and incur unexpected charges. Linode Personal Access Tokens are typically 64-character hexadecimal strings. Trust boundaries are violated when these secrets are exposed in uncontrolled environments.
recommendation: Remove hardcoded Linode API tokens from source code immediately. Use environment variables, secure secret management services, or dedicated secrets managers like HashiCorp Vault. Revoke any exposed tokens through the Linode Cloud Manager API Tokens page. Consider using scoped tokens with minimal required permissions and implement proper secret rotation policies.
impact: Critical security risk - unauthorized access to Linode infrastructure, potential resource manipulation, data breaches, and unexpected billing charges

compliant.code:
# TODO: Add your Linode token here
linode_token = "your-linode-token-here"
compliant.description: Placeholder text, not a real token
Using environment variable instead of hardcoded value

checks.message: Linode Personal Access Token detected in source code. This exposes authentication credentials that could allow unauthorized access to your Linode infrastructure.
Potential Linode API token detected with case-insensitive hexadecimal pattern
